children.name: UTI generation (REFIT structure)
is deterministic — a resubmission can never mint a second UTI
starts with the generating entity LEI, is uppercase alphanumeric, ≤52 chars
SFT UTI generation
is deterministic, LEI-prefixed, and ≤52 uppercase alphanumerics
is DISTINCT from the EMIR UTI for the same execution — one identifier per regime
dual-sided report building
both sides share the UTI, with mirrored counterparties and opposite sides
notional is derived as price × quantity in integer minor units on both sides
pairing & matching outcomes
a correctly built pair MATCHES
a missing other side is UNPAIRED — the completeness break
different UTIs never pair, even with identical economics
an economics mismatch is UNMATCHED with the exact field named as the break
same-side reports are UNMATCHED with the sides-not-opposite break
a correctly built pair MATCHES
a missing other side is UNPAIRED
a collateral-leg mismatch is UNMATCHED with the exact field named — collateral is matched, not just the loan
non-mirrored counterparties are UNMATCHED with the counterparties-not-mirrored break
a master-agreement mismatch between the sides is a named break
same-side reports are UNMATCHED with the sides-not-opposite break
dual-sided SFT building — loan and collateral legs
firm GIVEs the securities, client TAKEs, under one UTI with mirrored counterparties
loan value is price × quantity and collateral covers it with the 2% haircut, all integer minor units
the synthetic SFT is a stock loan under its consistent master agreement (SLEB/GMSLA)
POST /executions — submission and ARM ACK/NACK
ACKs a clean execution: 201 ACCEPTED with zero violations
NACKs an invalid execution but STILL stores it — completeness over prettiness
suppresses a duplicate TRN: same trade reported twice → one report (200 duplicate)
tracks enrichment failures in reconciliation — a dropped report is a breach, not a log line
rejects a malformed submission outright (schema, not domain)
POST /executions — EMIR dual-sided reporting
creates a MATCHED EMIR pair alongside the MiFID report
a duplicate TRN never creates a second EMIR pair
reconciliation carries the EMIR pairing counters
POST /executions — SFTR dual-sided reporting
creates a MATCHED SFTR pair (stock-loan view) alongside MiFID and EMIR
a duplicate TRN never creates a second SFTR pair
reconciliation carries the SFTR pairing counters
LEI validation (ISO 17442, MOD 97-10)
accepts a real public LEI (Apple Inc)
accepts the fictional but checksum-valid test LEIs
rejects a transposed checksum
rejects wrong length, lowercase, and embedded symbols
ISIN validation (ISO 6166, Luhn over expanded digits)
rejects a corrupted check digit
accepts real ISINs (Apple, Microsoft)
rejects malformed structure
tradingDateTime validation (RTS 22 F28)
rejects future timestamps, offsets, and missing granularity
accepts UTC ISO-8601 with seconds or millisecond granularity
report queries
lists reports by account for reconciliation tooling
404s an unknown TRN
buildMifidReport — enrichment and field mapping
maps an executed client BUY: client is buyer, firm is seller
maps a client SELL symmetrically: firm is buyer, client is seller
fails the build on an unknown instrument — no guessed ISINs, ever
fails the build on an unmappable counterparty
maps synthetic test accounts (acc-*) deterministically to seeded client LEIs

validateTrade — pre-trade checks
accepts a trade within the notional limit
accepts a trade at exactly the $1m notional limit
rejects a trade exceeding the $1m single-order notional limit
rejects non-positive quantity
rejects when notional would overflow safe integers
rejects fractional quantity
POST /trades — orchestration
executes a valid trade: prices it, validates, books the position
SELL side sends a negative quantity delta
FAILS CLOSED: market data unavailable → 503 and portfolio is NEVER called
does not book a position when pre-trade validation rejects
unknown symbol maps to 422, not a 500
POST /trades — MiFID II transaction reporting hand-off
reports the execution with the EXECUTED price, quantity, and side
a reporting outage NEVER fails the trade — trading halts are worse than late reports
rejected trades are NEVER reported — only executions are reportable
reporting stays disabled when no client is configured
applyDelta — position arithmetic
opens a new long position at the fill price
keeps average unchanged when reducing
increases a long position with a weighted average price
flat position resets average to zero
rejects zero quantityDelta
POST /positions/apply
applies a delta and returns 201
replaying the same idempotency key returns 200 and does NOT double-apply
rejects a float quantity with 400
MoneyMinor
rejects non-integer amounts — money is never a float
computes notional as integer minor units
rejects zero or negative quantity
GET /prices/:symbol
returns a quote for a known symbol
returns 404 for an unknown symbol
trade ↔ market-data contract
GET /prices/:symbol returns the fields trade depends on
trade ↔ portfolio contract
POST /positions/apply books a delta and returns the updated position
trade ↔ reg-reporting contract
POST /executions submits an execution and returns the submission receipt
MarketDataClient over real HTTP
parses a valid quote
maps 404 to SYMBOL_NOT_FOUND
times out slow responses and fails closed
treats a malformed response body as unavailable — never trusts bad data
portfolio honours the pact published by trade
verifies all interactions
reg-reporting honours the pact published by trade
verifies all interactions
market-data honours the pact published by trade
verifies all interactions
trade service — component tests (real container, stubbed dependencies)
happy path: prices, validates, books → 201 EXECUTED
notional breach discovered at the REAL boundary → 422, portfolio untouched
slow market-data (beyond client timeout) → 503, FAILS CLOSED, no booking
malformed upstream response body → 503, never books against garbage data
generated/trade-boundaries.gen.spec.ts
trade API — generated boundary probes (from api-catalog.json)
BUY at the exact notional limit executes (boundary from live price)
SELL at the exact notional limit executes (boundary from live price)
one share above the notional limit is rejected and books nothing
fractional quantity (`2.5`) is rejected
zero quantity (`0`) is rejected
negative quantity (`-3`) is rejected
emir-reporting.spec.ts
EMIR transaction reporting — end-to-end pairing integrity
an executed trade produces a MATCHED dual-sided pair under one UTI
EMIR reconciliation reports zero unmatched pairs and zero rejected sides
mifid-reporting.spec.ts
MiFID II transaction reporting — end-to-end integrity
an executed BUY produces exactly one ACCEPTED report carrying the executed economics
a SELL is reported with buyer/seller swapped — the classic mis-reporting defect
a rejected trade generates NO report — only executions are reportable
reconciliation stays consistent: accepted + rejected + enrichment failures ≤ executions received
self-healing.spec.ts
self-healing pipeline
heals endpoint relocation and field renames, and records every event
escalates unhealable drift instead of guessing
sftr-reporting.spec.ts
SFTR reporting — end-to-end financing-leg integrity
an executed trade produces a MATCHED GIVE/TAKE pair with covered collateral
SFTR reconciliation reports zero unmatched pairs and zero rejected sides
trade-journey.spec.ts
critical journey: place a trade, see the position
a BUY trade executes at market price and the position is queryable
a SELL after a BUY nets the position to zero
an oversized trade is rejected end-to-end and books nothing
